req.title: Reconciliation report
req.statement: The system shall produce a daily reconciliation report comparing internal ledger to correspondent bank; non-zero variance shall open an incident.

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-025 requires three observable behaviours: (1) a report is stored after each EOD trigger, (2) a non-zero variance opens an incident whose severity scales with the variance magnitude, and (3) a zero-variance run produces no incident. The violation in the previous response was test[1] being kind=negative with heuristic=EP — a negative test exercising an invalid/error partition must use heuristic=NEG, not EP. EP is reserved for valid equivalence partitions (positive). Fix: change test[1] (large variance opens incident) from kind=negative/heuristic=EP to kind=positive/heuristic=EP because a large variance is a valid, expected input that exercises the normal incident-creation path — it is a positive equivalence partition of the non-zero-variance class. The unauthenticated request test retains kind=negative/heuristic=NEG. All other tests are preserved unchanged.

tests.name: test_reconcile_zero_variance_stores_report_no_incident
preconditions: Internal ledger and correspondent bank balances are seeded to identical values
EOD cron job credentials are available via <fixture: cron_auth_headers>
actions: POST /cron/reconcile with a payload seeding zero variance via <helper_fn: seed_balanced_ledger>
expected: Response body field incident_opened equals false
trace: R-FIN-025: no incident on zero variance
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://banking-api/cron/reconcile"

def test_reconcile_zero_variance_stores_report_no_incident(cron_auth_headers, seed_balanced_ledger, fetch_report):
    seed_balanced_ledger(variance=0)
    resp = requests.post(BASE_URL, headers=cron_auth_headers, json={"trigger": "EOD"})
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert "report_id" in body, "report_id missing from response"
    assert body["incident_opened"] is False, "incident must not open on zero variance"
    report = fetch_report(body["report_id"])
    assert report is not None, "report must be persisted"
    assert report["variance"] == 0

tests.name: test_reconcile_large_variance_opens_incident
preconditions: Internal ledger seeded with a balance 10000.00 higher than correspondent bank via <helper_fn: seed_unbalanced_ledger>
EOD cron job credentials available via <fixture: cron_auth_headers>
expected: Response body field incident_opened equals true
Incident record exists with variance equal to 10000.00
Incident severity is HIGH or CRITICAL
trace: R-FIN-025: non-zero variance shall open an incident
R-FIN-025: incident severity based on variance
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://banking-api/cron/reconcile"
HIGH_VARIANCE = 10000.00

def test_reconcile_large_variance_opens_incident(cron_auth_headers, seed_unbalanced_ledger, fetch_latest_incident):
    seed_unbalanced_ledger(variance=HIGH_VARIANCE)
    resp = requests.post(BASE_URL, headers=cron_auth_headers, json={"trigger": "EOD"})
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["incident_opened"] is True, "incident must be opened for non-zero variance"
    incident = fetch_latest_incident(body["report_id"])
    assert incident is not None, "incident record must exist"
    assert incident["variance"] == HIGH_VARIANCE
    assert incident["severity"] in ("HIGH", "CRITICAL"), f"unexpected severity: {incident['severity']}"

tests.name: test_reconcile_minimum_nonzero_variance_opens_incident
preconditions: Internal ledger seeded with exactly 0.01 difference from correspondent bank via <helper_fn: seed_unbalanced_ledger>
EOD cron job credentials available via <fixture: cron_auth_headers>
actions: POST /cron/reconcile with EOD trigger and variance seeded at 0.01
expected: Response body field incident_opened equals true
trace: R-FIN-025: non-zero variance (boundary: smallest representable cent) shall open an incident
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://banking-api/cron/reconcile"
MIN_VARIANCE = 0.01

def test_reconcile_minimum_nonzero_variance_opens_incident(cron_auth_headers, seed_unbalanced_ledger, fetch_latest_incident, fetch_report):
    seed_unbalanced_ledger(variance=MIN_VARIANCE)
    resp = requests.post(BASE_URL, headers=cron_auth_headers, json={"trigger": "EOD"})
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["incident_opened"] is True, "incident must open even for minimum variance"
    incident = fetch_latest_incident(body["report_id"])
    assert incident is not None
    assert abs(incident["variance"] - MIN_VARIANCE) < 1e-9, "variance mismatch"
    report = fetch_report(body["report_id"])
    assert abs(report["variance"] - MIN_VARIANCE) < 1e-9

tests.name: test_reconcile_incident_severity_tiers
preconditions: EOD cron job credentials available via <fixture: cron_auth_headers>
actions: For each (variance, expected_severity) pair in decision table, seed ledger and POST /cron/reconcile
Capture incident severity from incident store
expected: Variance 100.00-9999.99 maps to severity MEDIUM or HIGH
trace: R-FIN-025: incident severity based on variance
R-FIN-025: non-zero variance shall open an incident
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://banking-api/cron/reconcile"

DECISION_TABLE = [
    (0.01,     "LOW"),
    (50.00,    "LOW"),
    (99.99,    "LOW"),
    (100.00,   "MEDIUM"),
    (5000.00,  "HIGH"),
    (9999.99,  "HIGH"),
    (10000.00, "CRITICAL"),
    (99999.99, "CRITICAL"),
]

@pytest.mark.parametrize("variance,expected_sev", DECISION_TABLE)
def test_reconcile_incident_severity_tiers(variance, expected_sev, cron_auth_headers, seed_unbalanced_ledger, fetch_latest_incident):
    seed_unbalanced_ledger(variance=variance)
    resp = requests.post(BASE_URL, headers=cron_auth_headers, json={"trigger": "EOD"})
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["incident_opened"] is True
    incident = fetch_latest_incident(body["report_id"])
    assert incident["severity"] == expected_sev, (
        f"variance={variance}: expected {expected_sev}, got {incident['severity']}"
    )

tests.name: test_reconcile_rejects_unauthenticated_request
expected: Response body contains an error message
trace: R-FIN-025: system job precondition — only authorised EOD trigger should execute reconciliation
R-FIN-025: error pathway — unauthenticated call must not mutate state
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://banking-api/cron/reconcile"

def test_reconcile_rejects_unauthenticated_request():
    resp = requests.post(BASE_URL, json={"trigger": "EOD"})
    assert resp.status_code in (401, 403), (
        f"expected 401 or 403 for unauthenticated request, got {resp.status_code}"
    )
    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body or "message" in body, "error message must be present in response"
